commands.review_summary:
The PR teaches effective reply routing to let internal `sessions_send` webchat handoffs inherit a target session's persisted external delivery route, with resolver and dispatch regression tests.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +141. Total +177 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not from a live run. Current main injects `sessions_send` as internal webchat and then suppresses route-reply for internal webchat unless an explicit delivery route is present, which matches the reported Feishu lost-reply path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should wait for contributor or maintainer live transport proof; there is no narrow automation repair because the blocker is evidence, not a patch defect.

target.title: [codex] fix(reply): preserve sessions_send external routes
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88044.

- preserve a target session's persisted external delivery context for internal `sessions_send` handoffs that enter through the internal `webchat` surface
- mark inherited external routes in `resolveEffectiveReplyRoute` so dispatch can route them without making `sessions_send` look like a Feishu/Telegram/etc. user turn
- keep ordinary webchat turns on their live webchat route, and ignore persisted webchat routes for inter-session handoffs

## Behavior

Before this change, an agent-to-agent `sessions_send` turn could arrive as internal `webchat` and then fail to deliver a reply back to the existing Feishu session, even when the target session still had a valid external `deliveryContext`.

After this change, only `sessions_send` inter-session handoffs with a persisted deliverable external `deliveryContext` inherit that route. Normal Control UI/webchat turns continue to use their live webchat route.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_send` replies can route through a target session's persisted external delivery context instead of being trapped as internal `webchat`.

Real environment tested: local source checkout worktree from latest `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: focused route resolver and dispatch tests pass in the PR worktree.

Observed result after fix: 198 focused Vitest tests passed.

What was not tested: live Feishu delivery against a real Feishu bot/account.
